comment: Manifest-driven plugins (issue neokapi/neokapi#438). The legacy plugins.json next to this file describes gRPC bridge plugins (Okapi); they are migrated to this schema in phases 8-9.

asr.description: Speech recognition (ASR) for kapi — transcribe audio/video to timing-anchored text via a bundled whisper.cpp (MIT)
asr.author: Neokapi <hello@neokapi.dev>

av.description: Video demux dependency for kapi — bundles LGPL ffmpeg/ffprobe so the in-process video reader can extract audio (→ asr) and sampled frames (→ vision OCR)
av.author: Neokapi <hello@neokapi.dev>

check.description: ML voice/style-similarity and register checks via a small multilingual sentence-embedding model
check.author: Neokapi <hello@neokapi.dev>

llm.description: Local LLM plugin — runs Google Gemma 4 (E2B) ONNX models in-process for on-device text generation with text, image, and audio input. A free, private alternative to paid cloud models.
llm.author: Neokapi <hello@neokapi.dev>

okapi-bridge.description: Okapi Framework filters (57+ formats) — IDML, HTML, XLIFF, PO, YAML, OpenXML, Markdown, …
okapi-bridge.author: Neokapi <hello@neokapi.dev>

pdfium.description: Read PDFs with correct text (incl. CID/Type0/CJK) and per-segment geometry via Google's PDFium
pdfium.author: Neokapi <hello@neokapi.dev>

sat.description: Segment text into sentences with the SaT (Segment any Text) ML model
sat.author: Neokapi <hello@neokapi.dev>

vision.description: Document vision for kapi — OCR now; ML layout and table structure in later phases (PP-OCRv5 ONNX models)
vision.author: Neokapi <hello@neokapi.dev>
